prompt: A traditional Chinese ink calligraphy artwork on aged beige xuan paper, presented as a clean flat scan or photograph of a single hanging scroll page with no frame. The composition is entirely vertical Chinese cursive script, bold expressive grass-script brushwork in deep black ink with dynamic stroke variation, flying-white texture, dry-brush breaks, and elegant rhythm. Write the full poem 怒发冲冠，凭栏处、潇潇雨歇。抬望眼，仰天长啸，壮怀激烈。三十功名尘与土，八千里路云和月。莫等闲，白了少年头，空悲切。\n靖康耻，犹未雪；臣子恨，何时灭！驾长车，踏破贺兰山缺。壮志饥餐胡虏肉，笑谈渴饮匈奴血。待从头，收拾旧山河，朝天阙。 in large vertical columns arranged right to left, about 8 main poem columns across the page, each column flowing freely with slight variations in spacing and tilt. Leave a wide left margin containing a smaller vertical inscription with 3 narrow columns: the title and attribution reading 岳飞 满江红 甲辰書月 某元, written in a more restrained but still cursive hand. Add exactly 3 small red seal stamps: 2 stacked square seals in the lower-left margin beneath the inscription, and 1 small oval seal near the upper-right edge beside the first main column. The paper should have subtle warm tan mottling, soft fibers, faint age marks, and natural uneven tone. Overall mood is heroic, scholarly, and museum-quality, with authentic Song-inspired Chinese calligraphy aesthetics, minimal background, high detail, and faithful brush texture.
